assembly_instructions: Makers-For-Life COVID-19 emergency ventilator in a ≈20×20×20 cm cube. The visualization is shown open-topped with the side walls removed so the pneumatic and electronic subsystems are inspectable. Base plate carries a front panel (with touchscreen, two alarm LEDs, a silence button, and a power button) and a rear connector panel. Pneumatic flow: air enters through an intake HEPA filter on the rear, feeds a brushless blower driven by its own motor on top, mixes with supplemental oxygen in the O2 mixer (fed from the rear O2 supply port), passes a flow and a pressure sensor and a proportional pressure valve, then out through the patient HEPA filter to the patient tube port on the front. Exhaled air returns through a separate exhaust HEPA filter and out a rear exhaust port. Electronics ride on a thin STM32 controller board in the rear of the case with a motor driver and power supply.

info.japanese_name: MakAir オープンソース人工呼吸器
info.summary: Emergency-grade, open-hardware ventilator designed by the Makers-For-Life collective during the COVID-19 pandemic. Targeted at hospital shortages in low-resource settings.
info.description: MakAir is an open-source emergency ventilator designed in spring 2020 by Makers For Life, a volunteer collective of French engineers, medical professionals, and makers responding to the COVID-19 ventilator shortage. It is a compact blower-based ventilator — a brushless blower draws ambient air through a HEPA filter, mixes it with optional supplemental oxygen in a passive mixer, meters it past a proportional pressure valve with in-line flow and pressure sensors, and delivers it through a disposable patient HEPA filter to the patient circuit. A separate exhaust-return filter handles expired gas. A touchscreen + two alarm LEDs on the front panel drive the UI; the STM32-based controller, motor driver, and power supply ride on the rear of the base. The enclosure is a ≈20×20×20 cm cube; CAD, firmware, and bill of materials are released under open licenses so hospitals can manufacture locally.
info.operator: Field-deployed by Makers-For-Life partners and hospital maker-programs in 2020–21
info.contractor: Makers For Life (volunteer French engineering/medical collective)
info.contract_value: Target ≈€500 in parts vs. €20k+ for commercial ICU ventilators
info.status: Open-source design; reference device certified for emergency use in several jurisdictions during COVID-19

facts.label: Architecture
facts.value: Brushless blower + passive O₂ mixer + proportional pressure-valve metering

facts.label: Control
facts.value: STM32 controller, touchscreen UI, two alarm LEDs

facts.label: Sensors
facts.value: In-line flow sensor + pressure sensor on the patient line

facts.label: Filters
facts.value: HEPA on blower intake + patient circuit + exhaust return

facts.label: Oxygen
facts.value: Optional supplemental O₂ via the rear supply port + mixer

sources.title: Makers For Life — MakAir on GitHub

parts.name: Base Plate
parts.role: Enclosure floor. Every internal module and panel bolts to it.

parts.name: Front Panel (touchscreen)
parts.role: Operator-facing front panel. Carries the touchscreen, alarm LEDs, and front buttons.

parts.name: Touchscreen Display
parts.role: Capacitive touchscreen — pressure/flow waveforms, alarm limits, modes.

parts.name: Alarm LED (High Priority)
parts.role: Red high-priority alarm light on the top-left of the front panel.

parts.name: Alarm LED (Medium Priority)
parts.role: Yellow medium-priority alarm light on the top-right.

parts.name: Alarm-Silence Button
parts.role: Front-panel button that silences an active alarm.

parts.name: Power Button
parts.role: On / stand-by button.

parts.name: Rear Connector Panel
parts.role: Rear panel carrying the O₂ supply port, AC power inlet, and exhaust port.

parts.name: Air-Intake HEPA Filter
parts.role: HEPA filter on the blower-intake line — scrubs room air before it enters the blower.

parts.name: Brushless Blower
parts.role: Centrifugal brushless blower — the prime mover that pressurizes the breath waveform.

parts.name: Blower Motor
parts.role: Brushless DC motor on top of the blower impeller.

parts.name: O₂ Mixer
parts.role: Passive mixing chamber — combines blower air with supplemental oxygen before the patient circuit.

parts.name: Proportional Pressure Valve
parts.role: Servo-driven proportional valve that meters the breath waveform into the patient line.

parts.name: Inspiratory Flow Sensor
parts.role: In-line flow sensor — feeds the control loop and tidal-volume calculation.

parts.name: Patient-Line Pressure Sensor
parts.role: Patient-line pressure sensor — feeds the servo loop and sounds over-pressure alarms.

parts.name: Patient HEPA Filter
parts.role: Disposable HEPA filter on the patient inspiratory line — swapped between patients.

parts.name: Exhaust HEPA Filter
parts.role: HEPA filter on the exhaust-return line — scrubs expired gas before it vents out the device.

parts.name: Tube — Intake → Blower
parts.role: Flexible silicone tube from the intake HEPA to the blower inlet.

parts.name: Tube — Blower → Mixer
parts.role: Tube from the blower output to the O₂ mixer inlet.

parts.name: Tube — Valve → Patient Filter
parts.role: Tube from the proportional-valve output to the patient HEPA filter.

parts.name: STM32 Controller Board
parts.role: STM32-based flight controller — runs the pressure/flow servo loop, alarm logic, and UI driver.

parts.name: Motor Driver Board
parts.role: Three-phase driver for the brushless blower motor.

parts.name: Power Supply
parts.role: Internal AC→DC power supply.

parts.name: AC Power Inlet
parts.role: IEC C14 mains inlet on the rear panel.

parts.name: O₂ Supply Port
parts.role: DIN O₂ inlet on the rear panel. Supplemental O₂ from a cylinder enters here.

parts.name: Exhaust Port
parts.role: Filtered exhaust port on the rear panel.

parts.name: Patient Tube Port
parts.role: 22 mm standard patient-circuit tube port on the top-right of the front panel.
